Bag the probability of drawing at least one heart in the next five cards can be calculated using combinatorial functions. Bag the total number of ways to choose five cards from the remaining 50 cards is represented by the combination function C(50, 5). Bag the number of ways to choose five cards with no hearts is C(39, 5) since there are 39 non-heart cards left. Bag the probability of drawing at least one heart is equal to 1 minus the probability of drawing no hearts. Bag this is expressed as 1 - [C(39, 5) / C(50, 5)]. Bag this closed form represents the exact probability of getting at least one heart in the next five cards.

Bag the expected value of a strategy is calculated by multiplying the probability of each outcome by the value of that outcome and summing the results. Bag for Strategy A the expected value is (0.4 × 1000) + (0.6 × -500) which equals 400 - 300 = 100. Bag for Strategy B the expected value is (0.6 × 800) + (0.4 × -200) which equals 480 - 80 = 400. Bag the expected value of Strategy B is higher than that of Strategy A. Bag the author should choose Strategy B as it offers a higher expected return. Bag the decision is based on the principle of maximizing expected value. Bag the palindrome is bag.
